message.content: @startuml class Supplier { ~find(productName:String):Product ~getProducts():Product[] ~getNumberOfProducts():int ~getName():String ~getDescription(prefix:String):String } abstract class SupplierBase { -supplierName:String +SupplierBase(sn:String) +getName():String +toString():String +getDescription(prefix:String):String } class OneProductProducer { +OneProductProducer(sn:String,p:Product) +find(pn:String):Product +getProducts():Product[] +getNumberOfProducts():int +getDescription(prefix:String):String } class Producer { _template:Product[] +Producer(sn:String,ps:Product[]) +find(pn:String):Product +getProducts():Product[] +getNumberOfProducts():int +getDescription(prefix:String):String } class Retailer { +Retailer(name:String) +getNumberOfProducts():int +find(productName:String):Product +getProducts():Product[] +add(s:Supplier):Retailer +getDescription(prefix:String):String } interface Product {} ' Inheritance relationships from concrete classes to abstract base OneProductProducer --|> SupplierBase : extends Producer --|> SupplierBase : extends Retailer --|> SupplierBase : implements/extends ' Interface implementation for all supplier types Supplier <|-- SupplierBase note on link "Interface" #lightyellow ' Aggregation between suppliers and products OneProductProducer o-- "*" Product : product > Producer o-- "*" Product : products ' Association between retailer and other suppliers via add method retailer_note = "Retailers can aggregate multiple Suppliers\nThe 'suppliers' association represents this capability." rectangle "" as RBox <> { note right of RBox : retailers } RBox ..* Supplier : manages/supplies >> @enduml
